metadata.name: pyjolt
metadata.summary: A batteries included async-first python webframework

modules.name: pyjolt.static
documentation:
Default static endpoint that serves all static files for the application
In production, static files should be serves directly by a reverse proxy server such
as Nginx. This reverse proxy server approach is more efficient

functions.name: pyjolt.static.get_file
documentation:
Asynchronously sends the file at `path`.
- `filename` is optional (used for Content-Disposition).
- `content_type` is optional (guess using `mimetypes` if not provided).

Returns a tuple (status_code, headers, body_bytes).

functions.name: pyjolt.static.static
documentation: Endpoint for static files

modules.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database
documentation:
sql_database.py
Module for sql database connection/intergration

functions.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.create_tables
documentation: Creates db tables with initilized SqlDatabase instance

classes.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.init_app
documentation:
Initilizes the database interface
app.get_conf("DATABASE_URI") must returns a connection string like:
"postgresql+asyncpg://user:pass@localhost/dbname"
or "sqlite+aiosqlite:///./test.db"

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.connect
documentation:
Creates the async engine and session factory, if not already created.
Also creates a single AsyncSession instance you can reuse.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.get_session
documentation:
Returns the current session. You typically call this inside your request handlers
or services to do queries, inserts, etc.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.commit
documentation: Explicitly commits the current transaction.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.rollback
documentation: Optional convenience for rolling back a transaction if something goes wrong.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.disconnect
documentation:
Closes the active session and disposes of the engine.
This should be called when your ASGI app shuts down.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.execute_raw
documentation: Optional: Execute a raw SQL statement. Useful if you have a custom query.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.db_uri
documentation: Returns database connection uri string

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.engine
documentation: Returns database engine

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.Model
documentation: Returns base model for all model classes

methods.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase.with_session
documentation:
Returns a decorator that:
- Creates a new AsyncSession per request
- Injects it as the last argument to the route handler
- Rolls back if an unhandled error occurs
- Closes the session automatically afterward

inner_classes.name: pyjolt.database.sql_database.SqlDatabase._BaseModel
documentation: Base model from sqlalchemy.orm

A simple async Database interface using SQLAlchemy.
It handles:
  - engine creation
  - session creation
  - explicit commit
  - connect & disconnect (dispose)

modules.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate
documentation:
migrate.py
Alembic integration for database migrations, with argparse subparsers for CLI commands.

functions.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.register_db_commands
documentation:
Registers subparsers (CLI commands) for database migrations to the app's CLI.
This replaces the old app.add_cli_command(...) calls with subparser definitions
so that we can parse arguments like "db-upgrade <revision>" or "db-migrate --message '...'",
etc.

classes.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.init_app
documentation: Initializes Alembic for the given app.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.get_alembic_config
documentation: Returns an Alembic configuration object.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate._copy_env_template
documentation: Copies the env.py template to the migrations directory.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.init
documentation: Initializes the Alembic migration environment (if not done already).

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.migrate
documentation: Creates a new Alembic revision script (autogenerated) with the given message.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.upgrade
documentation: Upgrades the database to the given revision (default=head).

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.downgrade
documentation: Downgrades the database to the given revision (default=-1, i.e. one step).

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.history
documentation: Shows the revision history.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.current
documentation: Shows the current revision of the database.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.stamp
documentation: Stamps the database with a given revision, without running migrations.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.heads
documentation: Displays all current head revisions.

methods.name: pyjolt.database.migrate.migrate.Migrate.show
documentation: Shows details of a specific revision (default=head).
Integrates Alembic with the application for managing database migrations.

modules.name: pyjolt.auth
documentation: Authentication module

modules.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication
documentation:
authentication.py
Authentication module of PyJolt

classes.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.__init__
documentation: Initilizer for authentication module

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.init_app
documentation: Configures authentication module

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.create_signed_cookie_value
documentation:
Creates a signed cookie value using HMAC and a secret key.

value: The string value to be signed
secret_key: The application's secret key for signing

Returns a base64-encoded signed value.

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.decode_signed_cookie
documentation:
Decodes and verifies a signed cookie value.

cookie_value: The signed cookie value to be verified and decoded
secret_key: The application's secret key for verification

Returns the original string value if the signature is valid.
Raises a ValueError if the signature is invalid.

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.secret_key
documentation: Returns app secret key or none

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.login_required
documentation: Returns a decorator that checks if a user is authenticated

methods.name: pyjolt.auth.authentication.Authentication.user_loader
documentation:
Decorator for designating user loader method. The decorated method should return
the user object (db model, dictionary or any other type) or None in the event of
unauthorized user.
Authentication class for PyJolt

modules.name: pyjolt.common
documentation: Common class which is used to extend the PyJolt and Blueprint class

classes.name: pyjolt.common.Common

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.get
documentation: Decorator for GET endpoints with path variables support.
Routing decorators [GET; POST; PUT; PATCH; DELETE]

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.post
documentation: Decorator for POST endpoints with path variables support.

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.put
documentation: Decorator for PUT endpoints with path variables support.

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.patch
documentation: Decorator for PATCH endpoints with path variables support.

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.delete
documentation: Decorator for DELETE endpoints with path variables support.

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common._add_route_function
documentation:
Adds the function to the Router.
Raises DuplicateRoutePath if a route with the same (method, path) is already registered.

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.input
documentation:
input decorator injects the received and validated data from json, form, multipart...
locations into the route handler.
Data is validated according to provided schema.

methods.name: pyjolt.common.Common.output
documentation:
output decorator handels data serialization. Automatically serializes the data
in the specified "field" of the route handler return dictionary. Default field name
is the DEFAULT_RESPONSE_DATA_FIELD of the application (defaults to "data"). Sets the status_code (default 200)

Common class which contains methods common to the PyJolt class and 
Blueprint class.

documentation: Adds a CLI command to the PyJolt CLI.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.run_cli
documentation: Executes the registered CLI commands.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.use_middleware
documentation: Add a middleware factory to the stack.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.add_extension
documentation: Adds extension to extension map

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.register_blueprint
documentation: Registers the blueprint, merging its routes into the app.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.exception_handler
documentation:
Decorator for registering exception handler methods. THe
decorated method gets the request and response object + any
path variables passed to it

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.before_start
documentation:
Decorator for registering methods that should run before application
starts. Methods are executed in the order they are appended to the list
and get the application object passed as the only argument

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.after_start
documentation:
Decorator for registering methods that should run after application
starts. Methods are executed in the order they are appended to the list
and get the application object passed as the only argument

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt._add_exception_handler
documentation: Adds exception handler method to handler dictionary

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.abort_route_not_found
documentation: Aborts request because route was not found

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.send_response
documentation: Sends response

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt._log_request
documentation: Logs incoming request

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt._base_app
documentation: The bare-bones application without any middleware.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.build
documentation:
Build the final app by wrapping self._app in all middleware.
Apply them in reverse order so the first middleware in the list
is the outermost layer.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.__call__
documentation: Once built, __call__ just delegates to the fully wrapped app.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.run
documentation:
Method for running the application. Should only be used for development.
Starts a uvicorn server with the application instance.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.get_conf
documentation:
Returns app configuration with provided config_name.
Raises error if configuration is not found.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.get_extension
documentation: Returns an extension by string name or object.__class__.__name__ property

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.root_path
documentation: Returns root path of application

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.configs
documentation: Returns configuration dictionary

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.routing_table
documentation: For debug/inspection: returns the underlying routing rules.

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.static_files_path
documentation: static files path

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.templates_path
documentation: templates directory path

methods.name: pyjolt.pyjolt.PyJolt.extensions
documentation: returns extension dictionary
PyJolt ASGI server class, now using a Router for advanced path matching.

functions.name: pyjolt.utilities.get_app_root_path
documentation:
Finds the root path of the application package on the file system or
uses the current working directory

modules.name: pyjolt
documentation: Init file for PyJolt package

modules.name: pyjolt.blueprint
documentation:
Class for creating endpoints which are logically grouped together in a file (blueprint).
The Blueprint instance is registered with the application and it's endpoints are added to the
application. Blueprints can be configures with url prefixes and some other configurations (see docs)

classes.name: pyjolt.blueprint.Blueprint

methods.name: pyjolt.blueprint.Blueprint.__init__
documentation: Initilizer for blueprint
Blueprint class

modules.name: pyjolt.router
documentation: Router class for application routing. Uses Wrkzeug under the hood.

classes.name: pyjolt.router.Router

methods.name: pyjolt.router.Router.add_route
documentation: Registers a route by creating a Rule in the internal Map.

methods.name: pyjolt.router.Router.match
documentation:
Matches the path and method against the routing map.
Returns (endpoint_function, path_variables_dict) if found, otherwise (None, {}).
A Router class that leverages Werkzeug’s Map/Rule system.

modules.name: pyjolt.cors
documentation: CORS module

modules.name: pyjolt.response
documentation: Response class. Holds all information regarding responses to individual requests

classes.name: pyjolt.response.Response

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.status
documentation: Sets status code of response

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.json
documentation:
Stringifies json data and creates json response
with application/json content-type and default
status code 200

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.html
documentation:
Renders html template and creates response with text/html content-type
and default status code 200

template_path: relative path of template inside the templates folder
context: dictionary with data used in the template

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.send_file
documentation:
For sending files
Sets correct headers and body of the response

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.set_header
documentation:
Sets or updates a header in the response.

key: Header name
value: Header value

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.set_cookie
documentation:
Sets a cookie in the response.

cookie_name: Cookie name
value: Cookie value
max_age: Max age of the cookie in seconds (optional)
path: Path where the cookie is available (default "/")
domain: Domain where the cookie is available (optional)
secure: If True, the cookie is only sent over HTTPS (default False)
http_only: If True, the cookie is inaccessible to JavaScript (default True) <-- MORE SECURE

methods.name: pyjolt.response.Response.delete_cookie
documentation:
Deletes a cookie by setting its Max-Age to 0.

cookie_name: Cookie name
path: Path where the cookie was available (default "/")
domain: Domain where the cookie was available (optional)
Holds status_code, headers, and body to be sent back.
